name: Engine, CLI & MCP Server
description: Three related orchestration modules: (1) engine.py — top-level async orchestrator that loads config, instantiates and starts all configured sources and sinks, wires source events through correlation engine into story manager, manages full lifecycle (start all → run → stop all). Uses asyncio.Queue as internal bus and asyncio.TaskGroup for structured concurrency. Runs until explicitly stopped via signal or programmatic call. No external broker. (2) cli.py — CLI entry points via argparse: `chronicler start` (launches engine), `chronicler status` (reads JSONL state), `chronicler stories list/show <id>` (open/closed stories from JSONL), `chronicler replay <file>` (replays JSONL events through engine with event-time clock). (3) mcp_server.py — MCP server using `mcp` Python SDK over stdio transport, runs as a task within engine's TaskGroup, exposes tools: chronicler_status, chronicler_stories_list, chronicler_stories_show, chronicler_events_replay. Covers AC10, AC11, AC13, AC14, AC15.

types.name: EnginePhase
types.description: Lifecycle phase of the ChroniclerEngine. Transitions: CREATED → STARTING → RUNNING → STOPPING → STOPPED. Also CREATED → STARTING → ERROR if startup fails.

types.name: SourceHealth
types.description: Health status of an individual source connector.

types.name: SinkHealth
types.description: Health status of an individual sink connector.

functions.name: ChroniclerEngine.__init__
functions.description: Construct the engine with validated configuration. Optionally accepts pre-built sources, sinks, correlation engine, and story manager for testing. Accepts an optional clock callable for replay mode (default: datetime.now(timezone.utc)). Creates a bounded asyncio.Queue. Engine starts in CREATED phase.

inputs.name: config
inputs.description: Validated engine configuration.

inputs.name: chronicler_config
inputs.description: Full ChroniclerConfig from config module, used to instantiate sources/sinks if not provided explicitly.

inputs.name: sources
inputs.description: Optional pre-built list of SourceProtocol instances. If empty, sources are instantiated from chronicler_config.

inputs.name: sinks
inputs.description: Optional pre-built list of SinkProtocol instances. If empty, sinks are instantiated from chronicler_config.

inputs.name: correlation_engine
inputs.description: Optional pre-built CorrelationEngine instance. If None, instantiated from chronicler_config.

inputs.name: story_manager
inputs.description: Optional pre-built StoryManager instance. If None, instantiated from chronicler_config.

inputs.name: clock
inputs.description: Callable[[], datetime] for current time. Default: datetime.now(timezone.utc). Override for replay with event-time semantics.

error_cases.name: invalid_config
error_cases.condition: config fails Pydantic validation

error_cases.name: invalid_queue_size
error_cases.condition: queue_max_size is out of range

preconditions: config is a valid EngineConfig instance
chronicler_config is a valid ChroniclerConfig instance
If sources/sinks are provided, they must implement SourceProtocol/SinkProtocol respectively
postconditions: Engine is in CREATED phase
Internal asyncio.Queue is created with maxsize=config.queue_max_size
No I/O has been performed
No tasks have been started

functions.name: ChroniclerEngine.start
functions.description: Async method that transitions engine from CREATED to RUNNING. Loads persisted state from JSONL, starts all source supervisor tasks in a TaskGroup, starts all sinks, optionally starts MCP server task if mcp_enabled. Registers SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers for graceful shutdown. Transitions to ERROR if any fatal startup failure occurs.

error_cases.name: already_started
error_cases.condition: Engine phase is not CREATED

error_data.message: Engine can only be started from CREATED phase

error_cases.name: state_load_failure
error_cases.condition: Cannot read JSONL state file

error_cases.name: source_init_failure
error_cases.condition: A source fails to initialize (logged, source marked FAILED, engine continues)

error_cases.name: sink_init_failure
error_cases.condition: A sink fails to initialize (logged, sink marked FAILED, engine continues)

preconditions: Engine phase is CREATED
Called within an async context (running event loop)
postconditions: Engine phase is RUNNING (or ERROR on failure)
All configured sources have supervisor tasks running
All configured sinks are started
MCP server task is running if mcp_enabled=true
SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers are registered
started_at timestamp is set
side_effects: Starts network listeners for webhook/OTLP sources

structured_side_effects.description: Load persisted stories and correlation state from previous run
Sources may bind to network ports

structured_side_effects.target: Engine internal state: phase, tasks, signal handlers
structured_side_effects.description: Transition to RUNNING and register signal handlers
Log startup progress and any source/sink initialization failures

functions.name: ChroniclerEngine.run
functions.description: Async method that runs the main processing loop. Consumes events from the internal asyncio.Queue, passes each through CorrelationEngine.process(), feeds results to StoryManager.update(), then fans out to all sinks via asyncio.gather(return_exceptions=True). Runs until the shutdown event is set (by signal or stop()). Calls start() if not already started. This is the primary entry point for cli.py's `chronicler start`.

error_cases.name: unrecoverable_error
error_cases.condition: Fatal error in processing loop that cannot be caught

error_data.message: Engine encountered an unrecoverable error in the main loop

error_cases.name: event_processing_error
error_cases.condition: Individual event fails correlation or story update (logged and dropped, never crashes)

preconditions: Called within an async context (running event loop)
postconditions: Engine has processed all events until shutdown
Shutdown sequence has completed (stop() called internally)
Engine phase is STOPPED
side_effects: Processes events through correlation and story manager

structured_side_effects.description: Events are correlated and stories are updated
Fan-out emits to all configured sinks

structured_side_effects.target: JSONL state file and disk sink
structured_side_effects.description: Persist state changes and emit events to disk sink
Log event processing, errors, and lifecycle events

performance_budget.complexity: O(S) per event where S = number of sinks

functions.name: ChroniclerEngine.stop
functions.description: Async method that initiates graceful shutdown. Transitions to STOPPING phase. Stops all sources (no new events). Drains remaining events from the queue with a timeout (config.drain_timeout_seconds). Flushes the story manager. Stops all sinks. Persists final state to JSONL. Transitions to STOPPED.

error_cases.name: already_stopped
error_cases.condition: Engine phase is STOPPED or CREATED

error_data.message: Engine is not in a stoppable phase

error_cases.name: drain_timeout
error_cases.condition: Queue did not drain within drain_timeout_seconds (remaining events logged and dropped)

error_cases.name: state_persist_failure
error_cases.condition: Cannot write final state to JSONL (logged, not fatal)

preconditions: Engine phase is RUNNING or STARTING
postconditions: Engine phase is STOPPED
All source tasks are cancelled and awaited
Queue is drained (or timed out)
Story manager has been flushed
All sinks are stopped
Final state is persisted to JSONL
Signal handlers are deregistered

structured_side_effects.description: Persist final engine state, open stories, and correlation state
Transition through STOPPING to STOPPED
Log shutdown progress, drain status, and any errors

functions.name: ChroniclerEngine.status
functions.description: Returns a snapshot of the engine's current status as an EngineStatus struct. Thread-safe and lock-free (reads atomic counters). Can be called from any phase.
postconditions: Returned EngineStatus reflects the engine state at the time of the call
No state mutation occurs

functions.name: ChroniclerEngine.submit_event
functions.description: Async method to programmatically submit an event to the engine's internal queue. Used by sources and by replay. Applies back-pressure if queue is full (blocks until space is available or timeout).

inputs.name: event
inputs.description: An Event instance (from schemas module) to submit for processing.

error_cases.condition: Engine phase is not RUNNING

error_cases.name: invalid_event
error_cases.condition: Event fails validation

preconditions: Engine phase is RUNNING
event is a valid Event instance
postconditions: Event is enqueued in the internal asyncio.Queue (returns true)
Or queue was full and timeout expired (returns false, event is dropped)

structured_side_effects.description: Enqueue event for processing by the main loop

functions.name: ChroniclerEngine.__aenter__
functions.description: Async context manager entry. Calls start() and returns self. Enables `async with ChroniclerEngine(...) as engine:` usage pattern.

error_cases.name: start_failure
error_cases.condition: start() raises an error

preconditions: Engine phase is CREATED
postconditions: Engine phase is RUNNING
Returns self

functions.name: ChroniclerEngine.__aexit__
functions.description: Async context manager exit. Calls stop() to perform graceful shutdown regardless of exception state.

inputs.name: exc_type
inputs.description: Exception type if context exited with exception, else None.

inputs.name: exc_val
inputs.description: Exception value if context exited with exception, else None.

inputs.name: exc_tb
inputs.description: Exception traceback if context exited with exception, else None.

postconditions: Engine phase is STOPPED
Returns False (exceptions are not suppressed)

structured_side_effects.description: Delegated to stop()
Delegated to stop()

functions.name: cli_cmd_start
functions.description: Handler for `chronicler start`. Loads config from YAML (default: chronicler.yaml, overridable via --config), constructs ChroniclerEngine, calls asyncio.run(engine.run()). Blocks until engine stops (via signal or error).

functions.name: cli_cmd_status
functions.description: Handler for `chronicler status`. Reads the JSONL state file directly (no running engine required) using the state module. Reconstructs and prints the engine status including last-known phase, event counts, and story counts. Outputs JSON to stdout.

functions.name: cli_cmd_replay
functions.description: Handler for `chronicler replay <file>`. Constructs engine in replay mode with clock=event_time and optionally suppressed sinks. Reads the JSONL file line by line, parses each as an Event, submits to the engine. Uses event timestamps as the clock source for deterministic replay. Returns a ReplayResult summary.

inputs.name: file_path
inputs.description: Path to the JSONL file to replay.

inputs.name: config_path
inputs.description: Path to YAML config for engine construction.

inputs.name: suppress_sinks
inputs.description: If true, suppress all sink emissions (dry-run mode).

functions.name: mcp_server_create
functions.description: Create and configure the MCP server instance. Registers all four tools (chronicler_status, chronicler_stories_list, chronicler_stories_show, chronicler_events_replay) with the mcp SDK. Binds tool handlers to engine methods. Returns the configured server ready to run over stdio transport.

functions.description: MCP tool handler for chronicler_events_replay. Accepts file_path and optional suppress_sinks parameters. Constructs a replay pipeline and processes the file through the running engine. Returns ReplayResult as McpToolResult. Note: replay through MCP runs within the live engine, using event timestamps for clock but processing through the existing correlation/story pipeline.

postconditions: Returns McpToolResult with success=true and ReplayResult data on success

structured_side_effects.target: Engine internal queue and story state
structured_side_effects.description: Events are submitted to the running engine's processing pipeline

performance_budget.complexity: O(N) where N = number of lines in the file

invariants: Engine phase transitions are strictly ordered: CREATED → STARTING → RUNNING → STOPPING → STOPPED, with CREATED → STARTING → ERROR as the only alternative path
The internal asyncio.Queue is bounded by config.queue_max_size; back-pressure is applied when full
Sources never crash the engine: all source exceptions are caught by supervisor coroutines, logged, and optionally restarted after source_restart_delay_seconds
Sinks are fire-and-forget: sink errors are logged but never block the processing pipeline or crash the engine
Invalid events are logged and dropped during correlation — they never crash the engine or corrupt story state
Shutdown always follows the order: stop sources → drain queue (with timeout) → flush story manager → stop sinks → persist final state
The engine's clock is injected and deterministic in replay mode — event timestamps drive time-dependent logic
JSONL state file is append-only during normal operation; full state can be reconstructed by replaying the log
CLI status and stories commands read JSONL state files directly without requiring a running engine
MCP server lifecycle is tied to the engine: it starts with the engine and stops when the engine stops
All I/O operations use async/await (asyncio); no blocking I/O on the event loop
Config validation fails fast at startup — invalid configuration is a fatal error that prevents engine creation

data_access.rationale: The engine reads INTERNAL-tier data from JSONL state files to reconstruct persisted story and correlation state on startup and for CLI offline queries. It reads PUBLIC-tier data from configured sources (webhook payloads, OTLP spans, file tails, Sentinel incidents). It writes INTERNAL-tier data to JSONL state files to persist engine state, open/closed stories, and event correlation mappings. The engine does not directly handle PII — any PII in event payloads passes through opaquely and is the responsibility of source/sink components and correlation rules to classify.

side_effects.rationale: Read JSONL state file on startup to restore persisted stories, correlation state, and event counters from previous engine run
Append to JSONL state file during operation and on shutdown to persist stories, correlation state, event counters, and final engine phase for recovery
Read YAML configuration file at startup and for CLI commands to load source/sink/correlation/engine settings
Read JSONL replay input files containing event records for the replay command
Sources bind to network ports (webhook HTTP, OTLP gRPC) to receive incoming events from external systems
Sinks emit processed events and stories to external systems (Stigmergy, Apprentice, Kindex) over HTTP
MCP server reads JSON-RPC requests from stdin and writes responses to stdout for tool invocations

authority.rationale: This component is the authoritative owner of engine lifecycle state (phase transitions, start/stop), engine status reporting (uptime, counters, source/sink health), and the event processing pipeline (queue → correlation → story manager → sink fan-out). It is the composition root that orchestrates all other Chronicler components. CLI and MCP server are thin interfaces that delegate to engine methods or read from the state files that the engine owns.
